case_title: ZHIXIONG LU VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES OF THE STATE
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: MINI MINUTES FOR APR-11-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE IS CONTINUED TO JUL-25-2016 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE NOTICE THE MATTER WOULD BE CONTESTED. THE MATTER IS DISCUSSED AND THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO JULY 25, 2016 FOR HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND STATUS OF THE PETITION. BY JULY 11, 2016, PETITIONER IS TO LODGE THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD AND A FILE MEMO STATING WHY PETITIONER BELIEVES THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE IS WRONG, AND WHAT RELIEF IS BEING SOUGHT. PETITIONER IS TO PROVIDE COPIES OF THESE DOCUMENTS TO MR. HAMILTON. IF PETITIONER TAKES THESE NEXT STEPS, THE COURT WILL SET A BRIEFING SCHEDULE FOR THE PETITION AT THE JULY 25, 2016 HEARING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR 5976

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

DEFENDANT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Dismiss Failure To Prosecute is Off calendar. The case was dismissed on September 22, 2016.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Continued To Sep-27-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney Zhixiong Lu appears In Propria Persona as petitioner/opposing party

Deputy Attorney General, Raymond W. Hamilton the California Justice Department appears as counsel for respondent/moving party, California Department of Motor Vehicles

Certified Interpreter Dorothy Wong, sowrn, interprets English/Cantonese for the petitioner.

The Court hears from both sides. The Court finds that petitioner wishes to continue prosecuting this action but requires more time to properly do so, given he is In Pro Per. The Court grants A 60-day continuance. Petitioner/opposing party is instructed and required to lodge with this Court a Department of Motor Vehicles Administrative Record and lodge a memorandum in opposition, and to provide a copy to respondent's counsel, Mr. Hamilton.

DEFENDANT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES OF THE STATE of CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Dismiss FOR Failure To Prosecute is continued to September 27, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Deparmtent 302.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar Apr-11-2016 Continued To Jul-25-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE.

Appearances: ZhiXiong Lu (415.823.1300) petitioner and opposing party appearing in propria persona with the assistance of Court Certified Interpreter Edward Chu (300949) in the Cantonese language; Raymond W. Hamilton of the Office of the Attorney General (510.622.2223) appearing for respondent and moving party.

The Court did not receive notice the matter would be contested. Discussed and the Court rules as follows: Continued to July 25, 2016 for defendant's motion to dismiss and status of the petition. By July 11, 2016, petitioner is to lodge the administrative record and a file memo stating why petitioner believes the Administrative Law Judge is wrong, and what relief is being sought. Petitioner is to provide copies of these documents to Mr. Hamilton. If petitioner takes these next steps, the Court will set a briefing schedule for the petition at the July 25, 2016 hearing. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR 5976
